title: Monotone constrained specialist for tyre-age and degradation physics
summary: Official XGBoost and LightGBM docs note that monotonic constraints can improve generalization when strong prior shape knowledge exists.
rationale: Official XGBoost and LightGBM docs note that monotonic constraints can improve generalization when strong prior shape knowledge exists. Pit risk should usually rise with tyre life/degradation and fall with laps remaining, at least within dry-regime subsets, so a constrained expert may generalize better across seasons than unconstrained trees.
implementation_hint: Train a separate constrained model on regime-filtered data or with regime interaction features, enforcing monotone increases for TyreLife, Cumulative_Degradation, and maybe StintProgress, and monotone decreases for EstimatedLapsLeft or RaceRemaining where appropriate. Use it as a specialist blend member rather than replacing the main model. Compare XGBoost constrained trees against an EBM/GA2M specialist with a few selected interactions.
expected_effect: Likely modest standalone score but potentially valuable as a drift-robust, low-correlation ensemble component.
risk: Wrong global constraints can flatten real strategy reversals, especially around wet tyres, mandatory compounds, or end-of-race non-pit zones.
